explicit_text: I was born in Rockford, Illinois, at Saint Anthony Hospital on April 25th, 1920. My early life was deeply connected to Rockford, where my father worked and where I graduated from Rockford High School in 1937. Later, I enlisted at Camp Grant in Rockford during World War II, marking a significant chapter in my life.

explicit_text: The Great Depression affected my family deeply; when I was 14, my father had just gotten a job again after the hard times. It was a difficult period, but my aunt helped me through it after my father passed away. The economic struggles shaped much of my early life.

explicit_text: After graduating high school in 1937, I worked in the office at Burson Knitting Company. I earned 25 cents an hour, but during the slack season and the following spring, I was laid off along with everyone else in the office. This job was a stepping stone before I entered nurse's training.

explicit_text: I was assigned to Percy Jones Hospital in Battle Creek, Michigan, in July 1943. I also participated in penicillin research there, which was groundbreaking at the time.

We also toured the island and visited Pearl Harbor and Hickam Field during our stay.
